test_cases.test:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
Px = np.random.randn(4, 10)
Qx = np.random.randn(4, 10)
mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2 = np.mean(Px, axis=1), np.cov(Px), np.mean(Qx, axis=1), np.cov(Qx)
print(round(multivariate_kl_divergence(mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2),4))

import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
Px = np.random.randn(3, 8)
Qx = np.random.randn(3, 8)
mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2 = np.mean(Px, axis=1), np.cov(Px), np.mean(Qx, axis=1), np.cov(Qx)
print(round(multivariate_kl_divergence(mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2),4))

example.input: mu_p, Cov_p, mu_q, Cov_q for two random multivariate Gaussians
example.output: A float representing the KL divergence
example.reasoning: The KL divergence is calculated using the formula: 0.5 * (log det term, minus dimension p, Mahalanobis distance between means, and trace term). It measures how dissimilar the second Gaussian is from the first.

starter_code:
import numpy as np

def multivariate_kl_divergence(mu_p: np.ndarray, Cov_p: np.ndarray, mu_q: np.ndarray, Cov_q: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """
    Computes the KL divergence between two multivariate Gaussian distributions.
    
    Parameters:
    mu_p: mean vector of the first distribution
    Cov_p: covariance matrix of the first distribution
    mu_q: mean vector of the second distribution
    Cov_q: covariance matrix of the second distribution

    Returns:
    KL divergence as a float
    """

title: Calculate KL Divergence Between Two Multivariate Gaussian Distributions

pytorch_test_cases.test:
import torch
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
Px = np.random.randn(4, 10)
Qx = np.random.randn(4, 10)
mu1, cov1 = torch.tensor(np.mean(Px, axis=1)), torch.tensor(np.cov(Px))
mu2, cov2 = torch.tensor(np.mean(Qx, axis=1)), torch.tensor(np.cov(Qx))
print(round(multivariate_kl_divergence(mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2), 4))

import torch
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
Px = np.random.randn(3, 8)
Qx = np.random.randn(3, 8)
mu1, cov1 = torch.tensor(np.mean(Px, axis=1)), torch.tensor(np.cov(Px))
mu2, cov2 = torch.tensor(np.mean(Qx, axis=1)), torch.tensor(np.cov(Qx))
print(round(multivariate_kl_divergence(mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2), 4))

tinygrad_test_cases.test:
from tinygrad.tensor import Tensor
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
Px = np.random.randn(4, 10)
Qx = np.random.randn(4, 10)
mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2 = np.mean(Px, axis=1), np.cov(Px), np.mean(Qx, axis=1), np.cov(Qx)
result = multivariate_kl_divergence(Tensor(mu1), Tensor(cov1), Tensor(mu2), Tensor(cov2))
print(round(result, 4))

from tinygrad.tensor import Tensor
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(42)
Px = np.random.randn(3, 8)
Qx = np.random.randn(3, 8)
mu1, cov1, mu2, cov2 = np.mean(Px, axis=1), np.cov(Px), np.mean(Qx, axis=1), np.cov(Qx)
result = multivariate_kl_divergence(Tensor(mu1), Tensor(cov1), Tensor(mu2), Tensor(cov2))
print(round(result, 4))
